reforms.description: The Swiss government has taken significant steps to support residential construction by granting a special credit of 400 million Swiss francs in 1971 and proposing a substantial increase in expenditure for housing development in the 1972 federal budget, aiming to alleviate housing shortages and promote equitable use of building resources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding aims to alleviate housing shortages, which can enhance living conditions and stimulate economic activity in the construction sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The special credit was granted in 1971, indicating implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at addressing housing needs.
reforms.source_quote: In March 1971 the Government granted a special 400 million Swiss francs credit for helping residential construction.
